summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the Resurrection Sunday service by celebrating the release of the church's new song 'Yahweh' and over 300 water baptisms on Good Friday. He then preached a sermon titled 'Yahweh,' exploring the profound meaning of God's name as revealed to Moses in Exodus 3. Pastor Travis connected this Old Testament revelation to Jesus, who declared 'I am' multiple times in the Gospels, emphasizing that Jesus is the same eternal God. The central message was that Jesus is 'the resurrection and the life' and 'the bread of life.' Pastor Travis shared his personal testimony of healing from a brain aneurysm and stroke, declaring that the God who raised Jesus also raises dead things in our lives today—be it health, marriages, or hope. He contrasted the 'bread of life' found in Christ with the 'bread of death' offered by the world, urging the congregation to stop looking for life among dead things and to consume the true sustenance found in God's Word, prayer, and worship. The sermon concluded with an invitation to accept salvation, and many responded to give their lives to Christ.
mainPoints: God's name 'Yahweh' (I AM) reveals His eternal, self-sufficient nature, and Jesus is the fulfillment of this name, offering Himself as everything we need.
Jesus is the resurrection and the life, meaning He not only rose from the dead but actively raises dead areas—relationships, health, hope—back to life for those who believe.
We must choose to consume the 'bread of life' (Jesus, His Word, prayer) and reject the 'bread of death' offered by the world, as what we feed our souls will either give life or lead to destruction.

versesMentioned.text: 'Do not come any closer,' God said. 'Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.'
'And now the cry of the Israelites has reached me, and I have seen the way the Egyptians are oppressing them.'
But Moses said to God, 'Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh and bring the Israelites out of Egypt?'
God said to Moses, 'I AM WHO I AM. This is what you are to say to the Israelites: ‘I AM has sent me to you.’'
'Very truly I tell you,' Jesus answered, 'before Abraham was born, I am!'

In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. The Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us. We have seen his glory, the glory of the one and only Son, who came from the Father, full of grace and truth.
So Jesus said, 'When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he and that I do nothing on my own but speak just what the Father has taught me.'

On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb. They found the stone rolled away from the tomb, but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus. While they were wondering about this, suddenly two men in clothes that gleamed like lightning stood beside them. In their fright the women bowed down with their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, 'Why do you look for the living among the dead? He is not here; he has risen!'
Then Jesus declared, 'I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.'
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Exodus 3:14 - 'God said to Moses, ‘I AM WHO I AM.'
Monday.action: Begin your day by declaring one 'I AM' statement of God that you need today (e.g., 'God, You are my Provider').
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank You that You are the great I AM. Help me to trust in Your eternal, unchanging nature today.

Tuesday.scripture: Luke 24:5-6 - 'Why do you look for the living among the dead? He is not here; he has risen!'
Tuesday.action: Identify one area where you've been looking for life or fulfillment in something other than Jesus. Consciously turn from it in prayer.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, forgive me for looking for life in dead places. You are the resurrection and the life. Be my source today.

Wednesday.scripture: John 6:35 - 'I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry.'
Wednesday.action: Spend an extra 5 minutes in prayer or reading Scripture, consciously 'feeding' on God's Word.
Wednesday.prayer: Bread of Life, nourish my soul. Satisfy my deepest hunger with Your presence and Your truth.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 11:14 - '...Satan himself masquerades as an angel of light.'
Thursday.action: Write down one temptation or lie you often face. Next to it, write the biblical truth that counters it.
Thursday.prayer: God, give me wisdom to recognize the enemy's deception. Help me to cling to Your truth as my defense.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 56:8 - 'Record my misery; list my tears on your scroll—are they not in your record?'
Friday.action: Honestly tell God about a current hurt or worry, trusting that He sees and collects your tears.
Friday.prayer: Father, I bring my pain to You. Thank You for seeing me, hearing me, and caring for me. I trust You with this.

Saturday.scripture: John 1:14 - 'The Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us.'
Saturday.action: Perform a simple act of kindness for someone, embodying the truth that Jesus dwells in and through you.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, You made Your home among us. Help me to make Your love and presence known to someone else today.
